behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the file path, function, commit message literal, --no-verify, --message, CalledProcessError, console_print, and run_command — all test specifics are discoverable.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes requirements (catch error, re-stage, retry with --no-verify, use --message) without providing exact code.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check structural requirements (try/except, --no-verify, --message, git add, console_print) that any correct fix matching the requirements would satisfy.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests use ast.parse to inspect structure and subprocess for compileall — verifying code structure rather than just substring matches; acceptable for this type of refactor task.

test_not_tautological.reason: Each f2p test requires specific structural elements (except handler for CalledProcessError, --no-verify flag, git add in handler, console_print in handler, --message flag) that a stub can't satisfy.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All instruction requirements (try/except, console_print, re-stage, --no-verify, --message, message format) have corresponding tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names and docstrings indicating pass_to_pass vs fail_to_pass.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names are consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points to the file and function but doesn't give exact line numbers or literal replacement code.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code fix task, not a config/agentmd edit.
